channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Minnesota union employees demand Walz halt half-time in office policy
item.description: Around 500 Minnesota state employees protested Saturday, calling on DFL Gov. Tim Walz to reverse course on a policy to require more in-office work. The governor says having more employees spend at least half their time at their workplaces will improve communication and collaboration.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: At least half of US states now outlaw devices that convert pistols into machine guns
item.description: In New Mexico, police and prosecutors backed an effort to outlaw devices that convert pistols into machine guns. In Alabama, the governor made it a priority.
item.dc:creator: DAVID A. LIEB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court upholds a Biden-era rule regulating ghost guns
item.description: The court says ghost guns, which are virtually untraceable weapons that require assembly, are considered "firearms" and subject to ATF regulation.
item.dc:creator: NPR Washington Desk, MPR News

item.title: France accuses US diplomats of meddling with a 'diktat' about Trump's DEI policies
item.description: A French minister has accused U.S. diplomats of interfering in the operations of French companies by sending them a letter reportedly telling them that U.S. President Donald Trump’s rollback of diversity, equity and inclusion initiatives could also apply outside of the United States.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Wisconsin's attorney general asks the state Supreme Court to stop Musk's $1 million payments
item.description: Wisconsin’s attorney general has asked the liberal-controlled state Supreme Court to stop billionaire Elon Musk from handing over $1 million checks to two voters.
item.dc:creator: Scott Bauer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: University of Minnesota leaders say ICE has detained a graduate student
item.description: The University of Minnesota says one of its graduate students is being detained by U.S. immigration authorities.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Media questions can shape Trump's decisions in second term
item.description: The White House is currently locked in a legal battle with the Associated Press over its decision to ban the wire service from key West Wing spaces over its “Gulf of America” stylebook policy.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says he won't 'fire people' over Signal messages, reiterates support of national security team
item.description: President Donald Trump is making his clearest commitment to not fire anyone over an embarrassing accidental leak of his administration’s plans for an airstrike against the Houthis in Yemen.
item.dc:creator: Chris Megerian, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Most US Institute of Peace workers get late-night word of their mass firing
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Most employees at the U.S. Institute of Peace, a congressionally created and funded think tank now taken over by Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency, received email notices of their mass firing, the latest step in the Trump administration's government downsizing.
item.dc:creator: GARY FIELDS and CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Richard Carlbom ascends to top role at Minnesota DFL Party as its first new chair in 14 years
item.description: Richard Carlbom was chosen as the Minnesota DFL Party chair, rising to the top post after years of working behind the scenes on party elections and causes.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Signal chat shows isolationist Vance as Trump flexes control abroad
item.description: The war plans group chat uncovered by a journalist at The Atlantic exposed some daylight between Vice President Vance and others in the administration.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's election order creates much confusion before the next federal election in 2026
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — President Donald Trump’s executive order seeking to change how U.S. elections are run is creating uncertainty for state and local election officials and worries about voter confusion before the next federal election, the 2026 midterms.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA A. CASSIDY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Musk says 'civilization will disappear' unless birth rates change
item.description: "The birth rate is very low in almost every country, and unless that changes, civilization will disappear," Musk said in an interview with Fox News.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Trump increasingly asks the Supreme Court to overrule judges blocking key parts of his agenda
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As losses mount in lower federal courts, President Donald Trump has returned to a tactic that he employed at the Supreme Court with remarkable success in his first term.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Why Trump's push for frigid Greenland is about icing out US adversaries
item.description: President Donald Trump’s push to acquire Greenland highlights the island’s growing strategic value, from rare earth mineral wealth to its role in Arctic defense.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Vance accuses Denmark of underinvesting in Greenland as Trump presses for US takeover of the island
item.description: NUUK, Greenland (AP) — U.S. Vice President JD Vance said Friday that Denmark has "underinvested" in Greenland’s security and demanded that Denmark change its approach as President Donald Trump pushes to take over the Danish territory. The pointed remarks came as Vance visited U.S.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP CROWTHER, KIRSTEN GRIESHABER and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Vance slams Denmark's handling of Greenland security
item.description: Vice President Vance on Friday slammed Denmark as failing to prioritize Greenland’s security, escalating rhetoric between Washington and Copenhagen amid President Trump’s claims to take over the island.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: The Minnesota Democratic-Farmer-Labor Party will elect a new chair on Saturday
item.description: Two Democrats are vying to lead the state’s DFL Party. Members of the DFL will vote Saturday for a replacement to longtime party chair Ken Martin who left earlier this year to head up the Democratic National Committee.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Kentucky Gov. Andy Beshear displays his colors in veto fights with Republican lawmakers
item.description: FRANKFORT, Ky. (AP) — Knowing the odds of winning were stacked against him, Democratic Gov. Andy Beshear nevertheless vetoed bills sent his way by the Republican-dominated Kentucky legislature with by-now familiar themes: abortion, Medicaid and diversity initiatives.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fewer Americans now see Canada as a close US ally as Trump strains a longtime partnership
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Americans are less likely to see Canada and the U.S. as close allies than they were two years ago, the latest indication that President Donald Trump's tariff threats and talk of taking over a neighboring ally are souring a critical economic and military relationship. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN and LINLEY SANDERS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Los Angeles Dodgers to make Trump White House visit next month
item.description: The Los Angeles Dodgers plan to visit President Donald Trump at the White House to celebrate their World Series victory, the team announced Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, Fox 5

item.title: Nevada Gov. Joe Lombardo talks possible Medicaid cuts, education, movie studios
item.description: Republican Nevada Gov. Joe Lombardo said he may have to call a special session later in the year to handle possible cuts from the federal government that directly impact the state budget.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Supreme Court seems divided over Louisiana case of congressional map with 2 Black majority seats
item.description: The Supreme Court seemed closely divided Monday over a challenge to Louisiana’s congressional map, which added a second Black majority district.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN and SARA CLINE, Associated Press, Fox 5
